column2: I moved an hour away from my long-term behavioral health clinic where I have medications and therapy. I am trying to find local outpatient services, but the places that call me have strange rules including: no one here will prescribe one of my two psychological medications, klonopin. I tried an alternative a few years ago, hydroxyzine, but did nothing for panic attacks. Recently benadryl was suggested, but it does not help anxiety or panic at all and caused severe dry mouth. also, neither hydroxide nor benadryl helped during manic episodes when these symptoms are more intense. 1) what is happening in which klonopin is no longer prescribed? 2) What are the alternatives that really work for moderate to severe panic attacks? my other psych is seroquel.

I live in Argentina, and I can't be demanding with my psychologists/psychiatrists because I'm broke and I can't afford to go for the private ones. I never got a diagnosis of what my problem is, in my teens I was prescribed 4 mg of risperidone, when I turned 18 I got 100 mg of quetiapine instead, and now I'm going to change again, to 5 mg of olanzapine... and honestly I'm probably not going to take it, it's frustrating not knowing what I'm taking this for apart from "most likely it will make you feel better." Quetiapine ruined my figure and made me earn more fat at the waist than anywhere else (I have a diet, avoid refined carbohydrates like the plague), and looking online, I found olanzapine does the same thing. I was considering mentioning my new psychiatrist who would prefer to take risperidone again, although I had problems with increasing my prolactin levels... but it's the least of two evils... this is why...
Please help me.

Hi I've never seen a psychiatrist before, so I'm not sure if it's the norm, but I and my son went on a date with an outpatient psychiatrist, I thought it was very unprofessional and rude. He said that if you think your son doesn't have a mental illness to go out when we ask for clarifications and question the diagnosis (I think he has a mental illness but not the correct diagnosis) he also told my son "that jumper is looking tight in you makes sand" which made him feel even more paranoid than he already is. He raised his voice a lot so I feel that other people in the waiting room could hear. I also feel that he was trying to provoke a reaction outside of my son. Is this normal and appropriate behavior?

I'm trying to understand my mother that, for about 25 years, she has had the firm belief that my aunt has been sending people - whether it's people my aunt knows or even my aunt herself - to spy on her at home, stalking her when she leaves, so that she can do less harm to her home, and more recently, her health. Retrospective story: 30 years ago my aunt's partner left her and moved with my mother and I until she could find her own place. my aunt was obsessed with her boyfriend leaving her and broke her mentally. she turned to religion but in an unhealthy way where she was no longer responsible for anything. good or bad, it was God. talking about her day, the weather, her vacation, her lunch, somehow she went back to God. she was also a bit of a sadist. she would insult you for no reason then laughing while she remained in the sequel of pain. she would lie about anything. she once medicated a medical note claiming to have help (26 years later she is alive and "well").

[24m] I need help to understand/fix a problem that my psychiatrist does not seem to understand tldr; he started having mild hallucinations 1h after taking my usual dose of quetiapine in July, first rarely and not scary, and now day by day and creepy. Why is this happening? I started taking quetiapine (400mg now, back to 300mg the following week) for bipolar disorder in March of this year along with alimemazine for insomnia. I take both 1h before when I want to fall asleep. It works well however in July I started to have mild hallucinations from time to time, always in the hour following my dose. Nothing crazy at first, the lines in the wood on the floor or the tables would slide slowly, or the same if there was a stain on a wall: it slides on the wall or moves slowly. In August, this type of mild hallucinations began to be more frequent, every few days and at this time I started to see whole objects moving too.

20f, 5'7, wear, Caucasian, 150lbs during the past week and half, I have felt incredibly restless. my mind has been running and jumping from one thing to the other at a very fast pace and transmitting a false sense of urgency to the point where I can't continue with it. I've only been cleaning my room and going on long walks, particularly at night, as a means of coping because I feel I need to be moving at all times. sleeping is very hard too. two nights ago I barely slept at all, and then the next morning ( yesterday) I had to get up at 7am for school, so I probably got a total of 5 hours of sleep, and that's to be generous. I had school until 9pm, and I still had a lot of energy so I went in an hour and a half walk after I was fired, I went to a friend's house after that (it was invited), and I got home around 12:30am. and I still managed to be up to 3 am barely packing around, cleaning my room, etc. I found that my energy is built throughout the day and hard.

I've been taking vyvanse for a while for adhd-pi, but I seem to get a bigger effect than desired that seems to pass much faster than I'm supposed to. at best it comes to me about half of 10-14 hours, even at higher doses. my resting heart rate goes up to 100-110 in the peak and this is my biggest concern, however, in the middle of the afternoon I can say a lot that it's getting worn out. it feels like it gets to something of a stable state for a few hours, but the stable state is a little too high, then the tanks and my heart rate falls along with it. pretty fascinating to see my heart rate chart on my watch essentially plot the concentration of the drug in my system so clearly. I just stumbled randomly through the cyp2d6 gene, and decided to look at it in my promethease report. lo and behold, it seems to me to be an “extensive metabolizer”.

I received the evaluation report today. confirmed the anxiety, with which I agree. however, I was not diagnosed with adhd. what I am curious about is the possible comorbidity of anxiety and adhd. I feel like a large part of the anxiety I experienced relates to issues I thought were connected with adhd (for example, making mistakes in work or not meeting deadlines, which leads to anxiety about negative consequences for these issues). also, I have noted "moderately over average" in the intellectual evaluation, and I am curious if I exceed the average.

I am also very energetic and happy or depressed and tired and I can't do any homework at all, I understand that studying can be boring at times but I'm really studying something that I'm passingsi

Unfortunately, mental health in the Netherlands is in the worst state and I have to wait a very long time to get something, so I want to briefly describe what is the situation in my case. I am m23 with anhedonia for life: since childhood, I have had difficulty getting pleasure from any intellectual activity that involves mental effort. I also struggle with experiencing *interest* (curiosity) on anything. however, I do not suffer from depression, and I am in excellent physical form. I participate in sports four times a week, maintain an extremely healthy diet, and sleep well. I simply do not experience pleasure at all, from social interactions and ending with reading books. I can only enjoy simple things like food, sleep, and video games (but sometimes have trouble enjoying games!), and nothing else. in other words, I can enjoy those, but anything related to mental effort is beyond me.

Hello r/askpsychiatry, 23 years old male here I am arriving today in the hope of finding some guidance and clarity in my situation. to give you some background, I started with a prescribed dose of 54mg concert for adhd. unfortunately, over time, I began to abuse it, which led to a cocaine, mdma, and addiction to the grass. Finally I stopped all the drugs and later fell into a depressive state. not being completely in advance, only informed my doctor that I had stopped the concert. in response, I prescribed 300mg of wellbutrin sr for my adhd and depression. however, I began to experience severe paranoia where I believed that everyone in my work was against me. to combat these effects, I was prescribed abilify: initially 2mg and later increased to 5mg. a few months down the line, I trusted my psychologist on my past drug abuse and asked him to inform my doctor. this led to my doctor sertraline 50mg. since then, I have made some positive life changes. - i, changed.
